claim_evidence_links.claim_text: Jeff initializes a workspace under `.jeff_runtime`, creating `project-1` / `wu-1` with no seeded run history on fresh or reset runtimes, while later startups reload persisted canonical state and support records.
The system does not start a GUI, daemon, or background continuation during startup.
Jeff is described as a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone rather than a GUI, a broad API surface, an autonomous continuation system, or a broad memo.

evidence_items.claim: Jeff initializes a workspace under `.jeff_runtime`, creating `project-1` / `wu-1` with no seeded run history on fresh or reset runtimes, while later startups reload persisted canonical state and support records.
evidence_items.evidence_text: Startup loads or initializes a persisted local runtime workspace under `.jeff_runtime`. On a fresh or reset runtime, Jeff initializes `project-1` / `wu-1` with no seeded run history; later startups reload the persisted canonical state and support records already present in that workspace. No daemon, GUI, or background continuation is started.

findings: Jeff initializes a workspace under `.jeff_runtime`, creating `project-1` / `wu-1` with no seeded run history on fresh or reset runtimes, while later startups reload persisted canonical state and support records.
The system does not start a GUI, daemon, or background continuation during startup.
Jeff is described as a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone rather than a GUI, a broad API surface, an autonomous continuation system, or a broad memo.
inference: The system relies on local filesystem persistence to maintain state between sessions.
